units.title: AutoChip
units.description: Generates functional Verilog modules from an initial design prompt and testbench using a selected LLM. Compilation and simulation errors are fed back into the LLM for iterative repair.
units.keyIdea: Iterative LLM-driven Verilog generation with EDA tool feedback loop for automatic error correction.

units.title: ROME
units.description: Uses hierarchical prompting to decompose complex designs so smaller open-source LLMs can generate larger Verilog systems with better quality and lower cost.
units.keyIdea: Hierarchical decomposition of hardware design tasks enables smaller LLMs to handle complex Verilog generation.

units.title: Veritas
units.description: Has an LLM generate CNF clauses as a formal functional specification and deterministically converts CNF to Verilog for correctness by construction.
units.keyIdea: NL → CNF clauses (via LLM) → deterministic Verilog synthesis ensures correctness by construction.

units.title: PrefixLLM
units.description: Represents prefix-adder synthesis as structured text (SPCR) and performs iterative LLM-guided design space exploration to optimize area and delay.
units.keyIdea: Prefix circuit synthesis as structured text enables LLM-driven design space exploration for optimal area/delay.

units.title: VeriDispatcher
units.description: Dispatches RTL tasks to LLMs using pre-inference difficulty prediction to improve quality and reduce cost. Routes each task to the most suitable model.
units.keyIdea: Multi-LLM routing via pre-inference difficulty prediction reduces cost while maintaining RTL quality.

units.title: VGen
units.description: Fine-tunes pre-trained LLMs on Verilog code from GitHub and textbooks. Evaluates them with syntax and testbench-based functional checks.
units.keyIdea: Fine-tuning on curated Verilog datasets (GitHub + textbooks) improves LLM Verilog generation with functional evaluation.

units.title: VeriThoughts
units.description: Provides a formal-verification-based pipeline to build a reasoning-oriented Verilog dataset and fine-tune LLMs for high-accuracy Verilog generation.
units.keyIdea: Formal verification grounded dataset enables reasoning-based Verilog generation with correctness guarantees.

units.title: VeriReason
units.description: A DeepSeek-R1-inspired RTL generation framework combining supervised fine-tuning with GRPO reinforcement learning and feedback-driven rewards.
units.keyIdea: RL with testbench feedback (GRPO) teaches LLMs autonomous error correction for Verilog generation.

units.title: VeriContaminated
units.description: First systematic analysis of data contamination in LLM-based Verilog code generation, evaluating major models on VerilogEval and RTLLM benchmarks.
units.keyIdea: Benchmark contamination analysis exposes validity issues in SOTA Verilog LLM evaluations.

units.title: Testbench Generation
units.description: Given RTL under test and a natural-language description, generates comprehensive test patterns refined using EDA tool feedback to improve coverage and expose bugs.
units.keyIdea: Iterative LLM-driven testbench generation with EDA feedback improves coverage for FSM verification.

units.title: Masala-CHAI
units.description: A fully automated framework leveraging LLMs to generate SPICE netlists for analog circuits, addressing challenges in automated netlist generation.
units.keyIdea: Multimodal LLMs (GPT-4) can automate SPICE netlist generation for analog circuit design.

units.title: Hybrid-NL2SVA
units.description: A RAG framework for NL2SVA and a fine-tuning pipeline with a synthetic dataset to train lightweight LLMs to translate natural-language properties into SVAs.
units.keyIdea: RAG + fine-tuning enables accurate translation of natural language properties to SystemVerilog assertions.

units.title: Security Assertions
units.description: Investigates LLMs for hardware assertion generation for security, using natural language prompts (code comments) to produce SystemVerilog assertions.
units.keyIdea: LLMs can generate security-centric SystemVerilog assertions from natural language code comments.

units.title: OpenTitan RAG SVA
units.description: Combines web scraping, semantic search, and LLMs to generate high-quality SVAs for OpenTitan hardware IP blocks using a RAG knowledge base.
units.keyIdea: RAG over OpenTitan documentation enables context-aware SVA generation for real hardware IP.

units.title: NSPG
units.description: A novel NLP-based Security Property Generator using hardware documentation. Introduces HS-BERT for extracting hardware security properties that convert to assertions.
units.keyIdea: HS-BERT extracts security properties from hardware documentation, automating assertion generation.

units.title: ATTRITION
units.description: A reinforcement-learning-based framework modeling a realistic adversary to evaluate and evade hardware Trojan detection methods, showing dramatically higher attack success than random insertion.
units.keyIdea: RL-based adversarial framework exposes weaknesses in static hardware Trojan detection techniques.

units.title: GHOST
units.description: An automated LLM-based attack framework that generates and inserts stealthy, synthesizable Hardware Trojans into HDL designs, enabling rapid Trojan creation.
units.keyIdea: LLMs can automatically generate and insert stealthy hardware Trojans, highlighting detection risks.

units.title: LLMPirate
units.description: LLM-driven rewriting to generate pirated circuit design variations that bypass state-of-the-art IP piracy detection tools, exposing a new hardware security threat.
units.keyIdea: LLMs can rewrite HDL to evade piracy detection tools, creating an automated IP piracy threat.

units.title: NOODLE
units.description: Uncertainty-aware Trojan detection using GAN-based data augmentation and multimodal deep learning with uncertainty quantification for risk-aware decision-making.
units.keyIdea: Multimodal DL with uncertainty quantification enables risk-aware hardware Trojan detection despite limited benchmarks.

units.title: TrojanLoc
units.description: Uses RTL-finetuned LLM embeddings and lightweight classifiers to detect Trojans, predict types, and localize suspicious RTL lines using TrojanInS dataset.
units.keyIdea: RTL-finetuned embeddings enable Trojan detection, type prediction, and line-level localization.

units.title: LockForge
units.description: A multi-agent LLM framework that automates translation of logic locking schemes from research papers into executable, validated code.
units.keyIdea: Multi-agent LLM pipeline (forethought → implement → refine → validate) converts LL papers to running code.

units.title: SALAD
units.description: Assessment framework using machine unlearning to remove sensitive IP, contaminated benchmarks, and malicious patterns from LLMs without full retraining.
units.keyIdea: Machine unlearning selectively removes harmful knowledge from Verilog LLMs while preserving model utility.

units.title: C2HLSC
units.description: Automated framework using LLMs to rewrite C code into HLS-synthesizable C, bridging the software-to-hardware design gap with divide-and-conquer hierarchy.
units.keyIdea: LLMs iteratively refactor C code into HLS-compatible formats guided by tool feedback and hierarchy decomposition.

projects.title: LLM-Based Verilog Adder Generation and Verification
projects.description: Explore LLM capabilities in hardware design by generating and verifying Verilog for digital adder circuits. Covers reverse engineering, LLM generation, and testbench-based verification.
objectives: Understand how LLMs can interpret and generate HDL code
Practice reverse engineering Verilog designs into natural language specs
Evaluate accuracy of LLM-generated Verilog against golden reference designs
Learn to leverage LLMs for automated testbench generation
Gain hands-on experience with hardware simulation tools (Iverilog)
